items.title: Enterprise buyers are experimenting with AI agents, but buying friction remains high
items.content_text: Enterprise buyers are piloting AI agents for support, operations, and back-office work. Security review, procurement review, and integration work still slow many pilots before full rollout.

items.title: Large platforms bundle native copilots and raise pressure on generic agent startups
items.content_text: Large software platforms bundled native copilots into their existing suites, increasing platform pressure on generic agent startups and pushing founders toward narrower vertical wedges.

items.title: Service agents win when they complete work, not when they just chat
items.content_text: Customers adopted service agents when the product completed useful work inside real systems. Teams that showed measurable ROI, renewal signals, or labor savings earned faster expansion.

items.title: Reliability and computer-use quality remain the main trust bottleneck
items.content_text: Latency, reliability, and accuracy issues still block broader deployment. Teams that invest in reliability improvements and workflow-specific quality gates preserve trust better than teams that expand too early.
